items.note:
[Backend] Workload sync should retry on failure
Context: the nightly capacity sync sometimes leaves a team with no numbers.
Impact: managers open the map to an empty state and lose trust in it.
Suggestion: retry the sync a few times before giving up, and show the last-good data meanwhile.
items.triageNote: Real defect that erodes trust in the map. Assigned to Wave 3 (Real Data Go-live) - sync reliability + last-good fallback belong to the data-go-live work.

items.note:
[Bug] Export button does nothing on the weekly report
Context: clicking Export gives no file and no error.
Impact: I can't share the week with my director.
Suggestion: it should download a PDF or show why it can't.
items.triageNote: A dead button is a real bug, not a nice-to-have. Moved from Testing to Assigned. The weekly report is an Executive-deck surface, so it belongs to Wave 1 (Executive Status Deck) to fix now.

items.note:
[Feature] Dark mode for the printed report
Context: the report is bright white when printed at night.
Impact: minor, cosmetic.
Suggestion: a dark theme for the report page.
items.triageNote: Valid but low-value and cosmetic, with no wave it clearly serves. Parked under Review - acknowledged, not inflated into a wave and not discarded. Revisit if it recurs or a print-polish wave opens.

items.note:
[Feature] Shareable weekly delivery digest
Context: directors want to forward "what shipped this week" without opening the app.
Impact: turns the page into a broadcast tool; less status-meeting time.
Suggestion: a one-click export (image or link) of the current week: % delivered, what shipped, what needs a decision.
items.triageNote: High-value and squarely Wave 1 (Executive Status Deck owns broadcast/reporting). Assigned to Wave 1.

items.note:
[Feature] Burnout early-warning alerts
Context: overload is spotted too late, usually after someone is already stressed.
Impact: protects people and lets managers rebalance before it becomes a resignation risk.
Suggestion: flag a person/team when sustained load crosses a threshold, with a quiet nudge to the manager (track work, never surveil).
items.triageNote: Serves Wave 2 (Capacity Engine) directly and aligns with the human-first principle. Assigned to Wave 2.

items.note:
[Feature] Auto-post "what shipped this week" to Slack
Context: the delivery story lives in the app; the team lives in Slack.
Impact: keeps everyone aware with zero effort and drives people back to the page for detail.
Suggestion: a scheduled Slack message summarising the weeks shipped items + a link, posted every Friday.
items.triageNote: Clean fit for Wave 4 (Slack Integration). Assigned to Wave 4.

items.note:
[Enhancement] Trend arrow on each wave card
Context: a wave card shows todays state but not whether it is getting better or worse.
Impact: lets a director see momentum at a glance, not just a snapshot.
Suggestion: add a small up/down arrow vs last weeks progress on each wave card (we already store history[]).
items.triageNote: Low-effort, high-signal improvement to the delivery view itself. Assigned to Wave 1.

items.note:
[Enhancement] Group the timeline by wave
Context: the timeline is chronological; sometimes you want "everything in Wave 2".
Impact: faster to answer "how is this wave doing" without scanning dates.
Suggestion: an optional "group by wave" toggle alongside the Week/All navigator.
items.triageNote: Legitimate but secondary to the filters we already ship; no clear wave owns a view-nicety. Parked at Review.

items.note:
[Frontend] Faster first paint on the Workload map
Context: the map can feel slow on first load with a full org.
Impact: better perceived performance; less waiting for the most-opened screen.
Suggestion: skeleton + progressive render so the shell appears instantly and data fills in.
items.triageNote: Valid polish but no wave clearly owns it yet and it is Low priority. Parked at Review, revisit under a perf pass.

items.note:
[New skill] Convert a feedback idea into a PR draft in one click
Context: today an accepted idea still has to be written up by hand to become work.
Impact: closes the loop from idea to shipped work; less manual transcription.
Suggestion: a button on a triaged item that opens a pre-filled PR/issue draft (blocked until the token-safe write proxy exists).
items.triageNote: Forward-looking and depends on the still-open write-proxy blocker. Parked at Review until that lands.

items.note:
[Backend] Monday director digest email
Context: directors do not always remember to open the page.
Impact: brings the delivery story to them at the start of the week.
Suggestion: a scheduled Monday email with the same digest as the shareable one (reuses idea-1).
items.triageNote: Depends on idea-1 and an email transport we do not have yet. Parked at Review, revisit after idea-1 ships.

items.note:
[Enhancement] Freshness + confidence badge on every derived number
Context: the page derives everything; a viewer cannot tell how fresh or solid a number is.
Impact: reinforces trust and the derive-never-assert principle - no number looks more certain than it is.
Suggestion: a small "as of <time>" / "based on N PRs" badge next to the headline figures.
items.triageNote: Strong trust alignment but a cross-cutting design task; no single wave owns it. Parked at Review for a design pass.
